back_translation: Therefore, I ask: did God (royally) reject His own people? Absolutely not — for I myself am also an Israelite, of the offspring of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin
God did not (royally) reject His people whom He (royally) foreknew. Or do you not know what the Scripture says about Elijah — how he appealed to God against Israel?
'Lord, they have killed Your prophets, they have demolished Your altars, and I am the only one left — and they are seeking my life'
But what did the (royal) divine reply (royally) say to him? 'I have reserved for Myself seven thousand men — those who have not bent the knee to Baal'
Therefore, in the present time as well, there is a remnant according to the (royal) choosing of grace
And if it is by grace, then it is no longer from conduct — otherwise, grace would no longer be grace
Therefore, what is it? What Israel sought, they did not obtain — but those whom He (royally) chose obtained it — and the rest were made hard-hearted
As it is written: 'God (royally) gave them a spirit of stupor — eyes that cannot see, and ears that cannot hear — until this very day'
And David says: 'Let their table become a snare, a trap, an obstacle, and a retribution against them
Let their eyes be darkened so as not to see — and let their backs be bent forever'
Therefore, I ask: did they stumble in order to fall and not get up again? Absolutely not — but by their transgression, salvation has come to the Gentiles, in order to provoke them to jealousy
And if their transgression is the riches of the world, and their loss is the riches of the Gentiles — how much more will their fullness be!
I am speaking to you who are Gentiles. Inasmuch as I am the apostle of the Gentiles, I therefore honor my ministry
In the hope that I may provoke my own people according to the flesh to jealousy, and save some of them
For if their being-rejected is the reconciliation of the world — what will their being-received-back be, if not life from the dead?
And if the firstfruit is holy, the whole lump is also holy — and if the root is holy, the branches are also holy
And if some branches were broken off, and you who are a wild olive shoot were grafted in among them, and have shared in the richness of the root of the olive tree
Do not boast over those branches — but if you do boast — you do not support the root, but the root supports you
Therefore, you will say: 'Branches were broken off so that I might be grafted in'
Correct — they were broken off because of unbelief, but you stand firm by faith. Do not think highly, but be afraid
For if God did not (royally) spare the natural branches, He will also not (royally) spare you

Therefore, consider the (royal) kindness and severity of God — severity to those who fell, but the (royal) kindness of God to you, if you continue in that (royal) kindness — otherwise, you also will be cut off
And those people too — if they do not continue in unbelief — they will be grafted in, for God (royally) is able to graft them in again

For if you were cut from the wild olive tree according to nature, and contrary to nature were grafted into the cultivated olive tree — how much more will those who are natural branches be grafted back into their own olive tree!

Brothers, I do not want you to be ignorant of this mystery — so that you will not presume yourselves wise — namely that hardening has come upon part of Israel, until the fullness of the Gentiles comes in
And in this way all Israel will be saved, just as it is written: 'The (royal) Deliverer will (royally) come from Zion — He will (royally) remove ungodliness from Jacob
And this is the covenant of Mine for them, when I take away their sins'
On the side of the gospel, they are enemies for your sake — but on the side of the (royal) choosing, they are beloved for the sake of the patriarchs
For the gifts and the (royal) calling of God are irrevocable
For just as you formerly were disobedient to God — but now have received (royal) mercy through the disobedience of those people
Therefore, those people now have been disobedient — through the (royal) mercy shown to you — so that they too may now receive (royal) mercy
For God (royally) imprisoned all in disobedience — so that He may (royally) show mercy to all
Oh, the depth of the riches, (royal) wisdom, and knowledge of God! His (royal) judgments cannot be searched out, and His (royal) ways cannot be traced
'For who has known the (royal) mind of the Lord? Or who has been His (royal) counselor?'
'Or who has given to Him first, so that He must (royally) repay?'
For all things come from Him, through Him, and for Him — let (royal) glory be to Him forever and ever — Amen
